meta.purpose: Cases showing prompt diagnosis in action — task mixing, goal ambiguity, context gaps.
meta.load_condition: Load when user asks for examples of prompt failure diagnosis.

cases.title: Marketing email prompt mixes four cognitive tasks
cases.context: A user's prompt: 'Write a marketing email for our new product. Evaluate whether it is convincing. Suggest three improvements. Then tell me which version to send.' The output is a well-formatted email with generic evaluation and vague improvement suggestions.
cases.what_happened: Without KDNA, the agent suggests adding role specification, audience details, and format constraints. With KDNA, the agent identifies four cognitive tasks in one prompt: generate (write), evaluate (is it convincing), revise (suggest improvements), decide (which version to send). The model defaults to producing a generic answer because it cannot optimize for all four simultaneously. Recommendation: Split into three sequential prompts — (1) generate 3 email variants, (2) evaluate each against specific criteria, (3) select the best one with rationale.
cases.what_was_learned: Task mixing is the most common invisible prompt failure. Adding more instructions to a task-mixed prompt makes it longer but not better. The fix is decomposition, not elaboration.
